findings.message: Uses `xargs -n 3 sh -c 'kubectl patch $2 -n $0 $1...'` with resource names piped from kubectl output; resource names are interpolated directly into a shell string, matching the HIGH shell injection pattern.
OAuth token cached to ~/.kubesphere_token (TOKEN_FILE) without setting restrictive file permissions; token is readable by other users on shared systems.
Common Issues troubleshooting table duplicated at lines 640–649 and 677–684; identical rows appear twice, bloating the file and creating a maintenance hazard.

API examples use `type: Opaque` which contradicts the explicit warning at lines 56–70 that Opaque breaks Jenkins credential sync; correct type is credential.devops.kubesphere.io/basic-auth or the matching domain-specific type.
'Project Components' ASCII architecture diagram block is duplicated; the same content block appears twice in the file starting at line 92.
'Key Resources' heading appears twice in the file, creating an ambiguous document structure and confusing section navigation.
Bold markdown marker is not closed: `**Step 3b: For Private Repository (with credential):` is missing its closing `**`; renders incorrectly and may confuse section boundary parsing.
Common Mistakes partial table at lines 1277–1282 duplicates content that appears earlier in the file.
Password 'P@88w0rd' hardcoded in documentation API examples at lines 130, 803, and 867; realistic-looking credential may be copied into production configurations.
The Authorization header `-H "Authorization: Bearer ${API_TOKEN}"` appears twice in the List Pipelines curl command (lines 247–249); the HTTP request contains a duplicate header.
Hardcoded example password 'P@88w0rd' at lines 130, 803, and 867 appears realistic; readers may use it in production without substitution.
YAML template syntax error: `low: "{{low}}` is missing closing double-quote; produces invalid YAML that will fail to parse when applied as an InstallPlan.
Identical to kubesphere-core/scripts/ks_api.py (file duplicated verbatim); OAuth token cached to ~/.kubesphere_token without restrictive file permissions; same insecure token storage pattern.

skills/kubesphere-core/scripts/ks_api.py and skills/kubesphere-multi-tenant-management/scripts/ks_api.py are byte-for-byte identical; security fixes must be applied to both files, creating a maintenance hazard.
Uses vague word 'appropriate' at line 713 without defining what an appropriate value means in this context; readers cannot determine compliance.
Placeholder comment says '# From Step 3' but the installation section defines only Step 1 and Step 2; agents will look for a non-existent step.

generate-config.sh reads OpenSearch credentials from the vector-sinks Kubernetes Secret (created by the vector extension) but this cross-extension runtime dependency is not documented in whizard-telemetry/SKILL.md or vector/SKILL.md; agents invoking the script without vector installed will get silent failures.
Prints OpenSearch password to stdout: `echo "  Password: ${OS_PASSWORD}"` where OS_PASSWORD is read from the vector-sinks Kubernetes Secret; credential leaks to terminal scrollback and shell logs.

API examples use `type: Opaque` which contradicts the explicit warning at lines 56–70 that Opaque breaks Jenkins credential sync; correct type is credential.devops.kubesphere.io/basic-auth or the matching domain-specific type.
YAML template syntax error: `low: "{{low}}` is missing closing double-quote; produces invalid YAML that will fail to parse when applied as an InstallPlan.
Placeholder comment says '# From Step 3' but the installation section defines only Step 1 and Step 2; agents will look for a non-existent step.
Bold markdown marker is not closed: `**Step 3b: For Private Repository (with credential):` is missing its closing `**`; renders incorrectly and may confuse section boundary parsing.
'Project Components' ASCII architecture diagram block is duplicated; the same content block appears twice in the file starting at line 92.

Uses `xargs -n 3 sh -c 'kubectl patch $2 -n $0 $1...'` with resource names piped from kubectl output; resource names are interpolated directly into a shell string, matching the HIGH shell injection pattern.
Prints OpenSearch password to stdout: `echo "  Password: ${OS_PASSWORD}"` where OS_PASSWORD is read from the vector-sinks Kubernetes Secret; credential leaks to terminal scrollback and shell logs.
OAuth token cached to ~/.kubesphere_token (TOKEN_FILE) without setting restrictive file permissions; token is readable by other users on shared systems.
Identical to kubesphere-core/scripts/ks_api.py (file duplicated verbatim); OAuth token cached to ~/.kubesphere_token without restrictive file permissions; same insecure token storage pattern.
Password 'P@88w0rd' hardcoded in documentation API examples at lines 130, 803, and 867; realistic-looking credential may be copied into production configurations.
Common Issues troubleshooting table duplicated at lines 640–649 and 677–684; identical rows appear twice, bloating the file and creating a maintenance hazard.
Common Mistakes partial table at lines 1277–1282 duplicates content that appears earlier in the file.
'Key Resources' heading appears twice in the file, creating an ambiguous document structure and confusing section navigation.
The Authorization header `-H "Authorization: Bearer ${API_TOKEN}"` appears twice in the List Pipelines curl command (lines 247–249); the HTTP request contains a duplicate header.
Hardcoded example password 'P@88w0rd' at lines 130, 803, and 867 appears realistic; readers may use it in production without substitution.
Uses vague word 'appropriate' at line 713 without defining what an appropriate value means in this context; readers cannot determine compliance.

skills/kubesphere-core/scripts/ks_api.py and skills/kubesphere-multi-tenant-management/scripts/ks_api.py are byte-for-byte identical; security fixes must be applied to both files, creating a maintenance hazard.

generate-config.sh reads OpenSearch credentials from the vector-sinks Kubernetes Secret (created by the vector extension) but this cross-extension runtime dependency is not documented in whizard-telemetry/SKILL.md or vector/SKILL.md; agents invoking the script without vector installed will get silent failures.
